pattern_recognition: Tree Traversal, Mirror Image Check - This problem falls under tree traversal techniques where we need to determine if a tree is symmetric. Signals include checking left and right subtrees for equality.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Recursively compare the left and right subtrees of the tree for equality.
approaches.algorithm: Perform a level-order traversal and compare nodes in pairs.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a recursive helper function to check symmetry by comparing nodes and their respective children.
approaches.algorithm: Check if the left subtree is a mirror of the right subtree recursively.

dry_run: Consider the input tree: [1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 3]. Start at the root (1), check left (2) and right (2). Both are equal, move to their children (3, 4) and (4, 3). Both pairs are equal, confirming symmetry.
edge_cases: An empty tree should return true. A single node tree should also return true. Trees with duplicate values can still be symmetric. Large trees should be handled without performance issues.
common_pitfalls: 1) Failing to check for null nodes correctly can lead to false negatives. 2) Not considering the structure of the tree (e.g., left and right children) can cause incorrect results. 3) Overlooking the symmetry condition for non-leaf nodes may lead to missed cases.
